data.acceptance:
1. A unit test asserts that running `doctor --fix` emits one status line per fix in the order fixes are applied, with each line containing the fix name and its result (ok/fail/skip).
2. A unit test asserts the final summary line (e.g. `N fixed, M skipped, K failed`) appears after all per-fix lines.
3. A CLI integration test runs `kno doctor --fix` against a fixture environment and captures stdout; output matches the expected ordered pattern.
4. A test asserts that `kno doctor` (without `--fix`) produces identical output to its baseline — no extra progress lines.
5. `make sanity` passes with no coverage regression.
6. A comment in the test file notes that flush timing (lines appearing before the next fix runs) requires manual TTY verification and explains how to do it.
